fault_hypothesis: The overlap condition on line 65 uses strict inequalities (`end <= s or start >= e`), which incorrectly allows touching boundaries (end == s or start == e) to pass without conflict. It should use `end < s or start > e` to treat touching as non-overlapping, or the test expectations require `end <= s or start >= e` to be changed to strict: the failing tests expect that touching boundaries ARE conflicts, so the condition should be `end < s or start > e` (i.e., no-overlap only when strictly separated).

spec.expr: not (end__1 == s) or not (end__1 <= s or start__1 >= e)

constraints.intent: When end equals s (touching boundary), the condition `end <= s` is True so no conflict is detected — this should be False for touching boundaries to be treated as overlapping.

spec.expr: not (end__1 == s) or not (start__1 == e) or False

constraints.intent: At each loop iteration, if end equals s or start equals e (touching), the function should have already returned False — but with the buggy condition it does not.

spec.expr: all((end__1 < s or start__1 > e) for s, e in existing) or not all((end__1 <= s or start__1 >= e) for s, e in existing)

constraints.intent: Before end__1 is used in the overlap check, verify that the no-overlap condition uses strict inequalities so touching boundaries are correctly identified as conflicts.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] is True and any(interval[1] == s or interval[0] == e for s, e in existing))

constraints.intent: The function must not accept an interval that touches any existing slot boundary.
